name: QueryFlux
description: AI-powered database intelligence platform with natural language querying, schema introspection, and query optimization.
author: QueryFlux Team

tools.name: execute_query
tools.description: Execute SQL query on connected database with safety checks and dry-run mode
examples: Run this query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status = 'pending'
Query the database: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 100

tools.name: get_schema
tools.description: Get complete database schema with tables, columns, types, and constraints
examples: What tables are in the database?

tools.name: natural_language_query
tools.description: Convert natural language question to SQL query using AI
examples: Find all users who signed up last week
Show me the top 10 products by revenue
What are the most popular categories?

tools.name: create_migration
tools.description: Generate SQL migration (up/down) from natural language description
examples: Create a migration to add an index on users.email
Generate a migration to add a created_at column to orders
Make a migration to rename the products table to items

tools.name: seed_test_data
tools.description: Generate AI-powered realistic test data for database tables
examples: Seed the orders table with 100 rows of test data

tools.name: explain_query
tools.description: Analyze query execution plan and get optimization suggestions
examples: Analyze this query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE email LIKE '%@example.com'
Explain the performance of: SELECT * FROM orders JOIN users ON orders.user_id = users.id
How can I optimize this query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM products GROUP BY category
